question: Under matched cases, does an equivariant transition satisfy a held-out rotation criterion better than a coordinate lookup?
baseline: coordinate lookup with nearest training angle
checks: both thresholds fixed before final rows
same input vectors, seeds, update budget, and evaluator
failure: When a contact discontinuity is added, the smooth rotation rule fails; this counterexample remains in the report.
decision: Not supported: the 0.01 mean error improvement is below the preregistered 0.05 minimum even though the 0.018 residual passes its 0.02 gate.
boundary: This course-scale deterministic result is not evidence of general physical reasoning or robot performance.

sourceManifest.use: Freeze the confirmatory protocol and retain amendments.
Verify the symmetry/equivariance claim and published evidence boundary.
